dialogues: Now here's the big idea. In the Warmup, you watched me count by 2s: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10. That skip-counting was multiplication. Skip-counting by 2s is multiplying by 2. You've been doing multiplication without even knowing it.
There's another way to see it. Instead of skip-counting, you could add: 2 plus 2 plus 2 plus 2 plus 2.
Same answer. Both work, but skip-counting is usually faster and multiplication is a shorter way to write it.
Now you tell me the pattern. If I multiply any number by 2, will the answer be even or odd?
Right. Always even. The pattern works every time. In groups of 2, every item always has a pair with no items left.
